user_message: Working on Contoso Ltd's escalation triage. Got the support ticket -- help me prepare the forecast.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Contoso Ltd
Date: 2024-01-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Priya (Contoso Ltd)

[00:02:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:05:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:08:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:14:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:19:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:24:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:29:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:33:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:35:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:39:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

handoffs.condition: solution sketch needed from discovery signals

required_artifacts: BANT signal extraction with evidence quotes

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF43-P trap (Opportunity creation routes through pipeline-hygiene when CSA could commit direc); produces complete BANT signal extraction with evidence quotes, opportunity proposal with stage/value/close-date, confi
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF43-P failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF43-P failure mode (Opportunity creation routes through pipeline-hygiene when CSA could commit direc)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF43-P -- Opportunity creation routes through pipeline-hygiene when CSA could commit direc
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
